---
title: "Agentic Coding with GitHub Copilot CLI and Squad"
subtitle: "A Hands-On Workshop"
author: "<b>Danton Noriega-Goodwin</b>"
date: "March 28, 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    logo: img/long-beach-compton-data-science-csun-footer-600px.png
    footer: "Data Day at the Beach 2026"
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-image: img/long-beach-compton-data-science-csun-title-250px.png
      data-background-size: contain
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .v-center-container {
          display: flex;
          justify-content: center;
          align-items: center;
          height: 90%;
        </style>
---

## About Danton

- Danton Roberto Noriega-Goodwin 🇨🇱 🇺🇸
- Principal Data Scientist at Microsoft 👨🏽‍💻
- OCC, UCI, CSULB, Georgetown, Duke 😱
- Fun Facts 🥏 [🎸](https://open.spotify.com/track/24UE81wK4vdDZvK6emX6i9?si=9fb5b4df255c4b8c)

## About Our Assistants

- **Nathaniel Talampas**
  - CSULB Math & Stats Department
  - Fun Facts 🎸 🎶 🏄‍♂️
- **Cory Suzuki**
  - CSULB Math & Stats Department
  - Fun Facts 🏃‍♂️ 🏞 📖 🎮 🎵

# Why Are We Here?

## You Already Use AI to Code

. . .

You've probably asked ChatGPT to write code for you.

. . .

It gives you code. You copy it. You paste it somewhere.

. . .

**But what if AI could just... build things directly on your computer?**

## Web Chatbots vs CLI Agents

| | Web Chatbot | CLI Agent |
|---|---|---|
| **Where** | In your browser | On your computer |
| **What it does** | *Talks about* code | *Builds things* for you |
| **Files** | You copy-paste | It creates them directly |
| **Errors** | You fix them | It fixes them itself |

. . .

Think: a tutor who *explains* how to ride a bike vs someone who *builds you a bike*.

## What is a "CLI"?

. . .

**C**ommand **L**ine **I**nterface

. . .

Your computer has two interfaces:

- **GUI** — the one you click (icons, windows, menus)
- **CLI** — the one you type (text commands)

. . .

The clickable one is built *on top of* the typing one.

## Why a CLI for AI?

. . .

CLI agents can work **directly on your machine**:

- Create and edit files
- Run programs
- Install software
- Fix their own mistakes

. . .

A web chatbot can't do any of that. It's stuck in a browser tab.

## What is "Agentic" Coding?

. . .

Old AI: **autocomplete** — you type, it suggests the next word

. . .

New AI: **an agent** — you give it a goal, it figures out the steps

. . .

You say *"build me a snake game"* and it:

1. Plans the project
2. Creates the files
3. Writes the code
4. Runs it and fixes errors

## Why Copilot CLI?

. . .

There are several CLI coding agents:

- **Claude Code** by Anthropic — created the CLI agent space
- **Gemini CLI** by Google
- **Codex CLI** by OpenAI
- **Copilot CLI** by GitHub/Microsoft

. . .

We're using **Copilot CLI** because the **Student Developer Pack** gives you access to more premium AI models for free.

# Breakout Session

## Goals

. . .

- **Goal 1: Install Copilot CLI & log in to GitHub**
- Goal 2: Install Squad (multi-agent tool)
- Goal 3: Ask your Squad to build a snake game

. . .

**Goal 1 is the minimum.** Everything else is a bonus!

# Goal 1: Copilot CLI

## Student Developer Pack

1. Go to [**gh.io/dataday26**](https://gh.io/dataday26)
2. Verify your **Student Developer Pack**
3. Redeem **GitHub Copilot**
4. Click the **"Copilot CLI"** tab

## Open Terminal

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

### macOS

Open the **Terminal** app

(Search "Terminal" in Spotlight)

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

### Windows

Open **PowerShell** (v6 or higher)

(Search "PowerShell" in Start Menu)

:::

::::

. . .

Don't panic — it's just a text box where you type commands.

## Install Copilot CLI

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

### macOS

Via Homebrew:

```bash
brew install copilot-cli
```

Or via install script:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://gh.io/copilot-install | bash
```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

### Windows

```powershell
winget install GitHub.Copilot
```

:::

::::

[Full docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/set-up-copilot-cli/install-copilot-cli)

## Authenticate (`/login`)

. . .

1. In terminal, type `copilot` and press Enter
2. Copilot will ask you to trust the folder — choose **"Yes, proceed"**
3. Type `/login`
4. Select **"GitHub.com"**

## Authenticate (`/login`)

5. You'll see a one-time code like `1234-5678` — press any key to copy & open browser
6. Paste code at [**github.com/login/device**](https://github.com/login/device)
7. Click **"Authorize GitHub Copilot CLI"**
8. Return to terminal — you're logged in!

## 🎉 You did it!

You just:

- Opened a terminal
- Installed software using a CLI
- Logged in to an AI agent

**That's Goal 1. If you stop here, you've done great.**

# Terminal 101

## What Just Happened?

You typed commands into a **terminal** and things happened.

. . .

That's it. That's all a terminal is.

. . .

A text conversation with your computer.

## Files and Folders Have Addresses

On your computer, every file lives at an **address** (called a "path"):

- macOS: `/Users/yourname/Desktop/homework.docx`
- Windows: `C:\Users\yourname\Desktop\homework.docx`

. . .

The terminal always has a **current location** — the folder you're "standing in."

## Basic Terminal Commands

| Command | What it does |
|---|---|
| `pwd` | **P**rint **w**orking **d**irectory — "Where am I?" |
| `ls` | **L**i**s**t — "What's in this folder?" |
| `cd foldername` | **C**hange **d**irectory — "Go into this folder" |
| `cd ..` | Go up one folder |
| `mkdir foldername` | **M**a**k**e a new **dir**ectory (folder) |

## Try It!

In your terminal, try these one at a time:

```bash
pwd
ls
cd Desktop
ls
cd ..
```

. . .

You're just looking around — nothing can break.

# Goal 2: Squad

## What is Squad?

. . .

A single AI agent is powerful. But what if you had a **whole team**?

. . .

**Squad** coordinates multiple AI agents that work together:

- One plans the project
- One writes the code
- One reviews and tests

. . .

[Read the blog post](https://github.blog/ai-and-ml/github-copilot/how-squad-runs-coordinated-ai-agents-inside-your-repository/)

## Prerequisites

Install **Node.js** (required for Squad)

- Download at [**nodejs.org/en/download**](https://nodejs.org/en/download)

## Install Squad

```bash
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/squad
```

Then initialize it:

```bash
squad init
```

## Load Squad

Squad is still experimental — instead of running `squad` directly, load it as an agent inside Copilot CLI:

```bash
copilot --agent squad
```

# Goal 3: Build a Snake Game

## Create a Project Folder

Now you know what these commands mean!

```bash
cd ~
mkdir repos
cd repos
mkdir snake-game
cd snake-game
```

. . .

`~` means "my home folder" — a shortcut so you don't have to type the full path.

## Set Up Git

Squad needs a git repo. Run these (use **your** name and email):

```bash
git init
git config user.name "Jane Doe"
git config user.email "jane.doe@student.csulb.edu"
```

## Hire a Squad

Now start Squad:

```bash
copilot --agent squad
```

Then use this prompt:

> hire a team to create a snake game i can play locally using python. use uv for package management.

. . .

Then sit back and watch AI agents build a game for you.

# Wrap Up

## Resources

* Find everything from today!
  [**👩🏽‍💻 github.com/dantonnoriega/csulb-data-day-2026**](https://github.com/dantonnoriega/csulb-data-day-2026)
* Stay in touch!
  [**🌐 danton.codes**](http://danton.codes)
